Building digital confidence & connection

RECONNECT-US

We support people on probation or leaving long prison sentences to safely access the internet, reconnect with family, and build skills for a fresh start.

Join the 6–8 Week Course → Ask a Question Volunteer with Us

About RECONNECT-US

Everyone should be able to participate online with confidence. RECONNECT-US supports people of all ages and backgrounds to build practical, safe digital skills — from video calling and messaging to accessing services, learning, and work opportunities.

If you feel left behind by technology, we’re here to help — at your pace, without judgement.

How we work

  • Practical: Real‑life tasks (appointments, accounts, video calls) so skills stick.
  • Respectful: Person‑centred support — we start from where you are.
  • Forward‑looking: We build confidence, independence, and hope.

Who we support

Older adults, carers, job seekers, community members, and anyone who wants to get more from the internet — safely and confidently.

Our commitment

Everything we do is guided by safeguarding, inclusivity, and privacy. We create welcoming spaces where everyone can learn.

What success looks like

  • Confident use of essential online services (NHS App, GOV.UK, banking)
  • Safer contact with family and friends
  • Progression into learning, volunteering, or work

Mission Statement

Our mission is to make digital life accessible, safe, and useful for everyone. We help people build the skills and confidence to connect, learn, work, and thrive online.

We believe digital access is a basic right. Through practical training, mentoring, and clear guidance, we remove barriers and open up opportunities.

Programmes & Services

Flexible options to meet you where you are — from structured courses to one‑to‑one support.

Everyday Digital Skills

  • Using smartphones, tablets, and laptops
  • Email, attachments, calendars, cloud storage
  • NHS App, GOV.UK, banking & utilities
  • Software updates & device care

Outcome: Everyday digital independence.

Online Safety & Privacy

  • Spot scams & phishing, check websites safely
  • Password managers & two‑factor authentication
  • Privacy settings on apps and social media
  • Healthy online boundaries & digital wellbeing

Outcome: Safer, more confident online use.

Staying in Touch

  • Video calling (WhatsApp, Zoom, Teams)
  • Private messaging & group etiquette
  • Sharing photos & memories safely
  • Backing up important files

Outcome: Stronger, safer connections.

Work & Learning Online

  • CVs, simple cover letters, email etiquette
  • Using job sites & alerts
  • Online applications & interviews
  • Finding free learning resources

Outcome: Clear pathway into learning or work.

Guides & Handouts

  • Plain‑English step‑by‑step guides
  • Printable checklists and worksheets
  • Group resources for community partners
  • Accessible formats on request

Outcome: Takeaway resources to reinforce learning.

1:1 Mentoring

  • Personal goals & weekly action plans
  • Confidence‑building & troubleshooting
  • Safe habits & digital routines
  • Warm handovers to local services

Outcome: Sustained progress and accountability.

Professional 6–8 Week Course

A friendly, structured course for adults who want more confidence online — whatever your starting point.

Structure

  • Weekly 2‑hour sessions over 6–8 weeks
  • Small groups of 8 participants
  • Clients should bring their own devices that they are used to.

Outcomes

  • Completion certificate and personalised next steps
  • Confidence using essential online services
  • Safer digital habits and problem‑solving skills

How to join

You can self‑refer or ask a community organisation to introduce you — we’ll help you find the right group.

Request a joining form Ask a question

Volunteer with RECONNECT-US

Give a little time, change a lot. We’ll train you and support you throughout.

What we look for

  • Patience and good listening
  • Basic digital skills (we’ll train you in our approach)
  • Reliability and professionalism
  • Safeguarding awareness and a respectful attitude

What you’ll do

  • Support small groups or 1:1 sessions
  • Help learners practise safely on real tasks
  • Celebrate progress and build confidence
  • Signpost to local opportunities
Register your interest

Policies & Guidance

We operate under a framework of policies designed to keep learners, staff, and volunteers safe and respected.

Online Safety Policy

We teach and enforce safe online practices, helping learners understand risks such as scams, phishing, and unsafe browsing. All activity is guided by safeguarding principles.

Reporting Online Safety Issues

Any learner, staff, or volunteer can report an issue at any time. Concerns are logged, reviewed quickly, and acted upon in line with safeguarding standards.

Confidential Waste Policy

We securely destroy or delete sensitive materials, following UK GDPR and data protection requirements.

Safeguarding

All staff and volunteers are trained in safeguarding. We prioritise learner safety above all else, with clear reporting routes for concerns.

Equality & Inclusion

RECONNECT-US is committed to inclusivity, ensuring all services are free from discrimination and accessible to everyone.

Terms of Operation

Accessibility: Our services are free from discrimination. Everyone has the right to fair access.

Safeguarding: We protect the wellbeing of all learners and volunteers, applying strict checks and training.

Confidentiality: Information is held securely and only used for service delivery, in line with UK GDPR.

Participation: Learners must treat staff and peers with respect. Unsafe behaviour will not be tolerated.

Transparency: Policies and processes are open and feedback is always welcomed.

Privacy Policy

Effective Date: [Insert Date]

RECONNECT-US is committed to protecting your privacy and handling your personal data responsibly. This Privacy Policy explains what data we collect, how we use it, how we keep it secure, and your rights under the UK General Data Protection Regulation (UK GDPR) and the Data Protection Act 2018.

1. Data We Collect

  • Identity details (first name, surname)
  • Contact details (email address, phone number, postal address)
  • Participation information (programme enrolments, referrals, attendance records)
  • Communications (messages you send us, enquiry forms, feedback, complaints)

We do not collect sensitive information unless directly relevant to delivering our services, and only with your knowledge and consent.

2. How We Use Your Data

  • To respond to enquiries and provide requested information
  • To manage referrals and participation in programmes
  • To communicate important updates (sessions, policy changes)
  • To ensure safeguarding, safety, and legal compliance
  • To improve services through anonymised feedback and evaluation

3. Lawful Basis for Processing

  • Consent: When you voluntarily provide data via forms or email
  • Contract: To deliver services you’ve requested or agreed to
  • Legal obligation: Where required by law or safeguarding
  • Legitimate interests: To evaluate and improve our programmes

4. Data Sharing

We will never sell your personal data. We only share information with trusted service providers or safeguarding bodies where necessary and lawful.

5. Data Storage & Retention

  • Data is stored securely in encrypted systems
  • Physical documents are kept in locked storage with restricted access
  • Contact form enquiries: retained up to 12 months
  • Programme records: retained up to 6 years

6. Your Rights

Under UK GDPR, you have rights to access, correct, erase, restrict, object, and port your data. To exercise rights, email reconnectus@outlook.com. We will respond within one month.

7. Updates

We may update this Privacy Policy to reflect legal or service changes. Updates will be published on this site.
